AWSAccountManagerReconcileRequest.description:
Request to reconcile a single AWS account.

If ``organization`` is present, the account is treated as an organization
account (payer credentials + role assumption). Otherwise it is a standalone
account with direct credentials.

automation_role.description: Payer's manager role ARN (required for org accounts)
automation_role.title: Automation Role

automation_token.description: Credentials (payer's for org accounts, own for non-org)

dry_run.description: If True, only calculate actions without executing.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

organization.description: Organization details (OU + tags). If set, account is org-managed.

organization_account_role.description: Role to assume in org account
organization_account_role.title: Organization Account Role

payer_uid.description: Payer account UID (required for org accounts — role assumed on payer, not org account)
payer_uid.title: Payer Uid

ChatRequest.description:
Request model for posting a Slack message.

Immutable model with all fields required to send a chat message.

Attributes:
    workspace_name: Slack workspace name
    channel: Channel name to post to
    text: Message text
    thread_ts: Optional thread timestamp for replies
    icon_emoji: Emoji to use as the message icon
    icon_url: URL to an image to use as the message icon
    username: Bot username to display
    secret: Secret reference for Slack bot token

icon_emoji.description: Emoji to use as the message icon (e.g., ':robot_face:')
icon_emoji.title: Icon Emoji

icon_url.description: URL to an image to use as the message icon
icon_url.title: Icon Url

secret.description: Secret reference for Slack bot token

ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest

ChatResponse.description:
Response model for a posted Slack message.

Immutable model returning the Slack API response fields.

Attributes:
    ts: Message timestamp
    channel: Channel ID where the message was posted
    thread_ts: Thread timestamp if this was a threaded reply

channel.description: Channel ID where the message was posted
channel.title: Channel

thread_ts.description: Thread timestamp if this was a threaded reply
thread_ts.title: Thread Ts

ChatResponse.title: ChatResponse

get.description:
Get users in a PagerDuty escalation policy.

Fetches all users across all escalation rules in the policy.
Results are cached for performance (TTL configured in settings).

Args:
    policy_id: PagerDuty escalation policy ID
    instance: PagerDuty instance name

Returns:
    EscalationPolicyUsersResponse with list of users

Raises:
    HTTPException:
        - 500 Internal Server Error: If PagerDuty API call fails

Example:
    GET /api/v1/external/pagerduty/escalation-policies/XYZ789/users?instance=app-sre
    Response:
    {
        "users": [
            {"username": "jsmith"},
            {"username": "mdoe"}
        ]
    }

Get users currently on-call in a PagerDuty schedule.

Fetches users from the specified schedule using a time window of now + 60 seconds.
Results are cached for performance (TTL configured in settings).

Args:
    schedule_id: PagerDuty schedule ID
    instance: PagerDuty instance name

Returns:
    ScheduleUsersResponse with list of users

Raises:
    HTTPException:
        - 500 Internal Server Error: If PagerDuty API call fails

Example:
    GET /api/v1/external/pagerduty/schedules/ABC123/users?instance=app-sre
    Response:
    {
        "users": [
            {"username": "jsmith"},
            {"username": "mdoe"}
        ]
    }

post.description:
Post a message to a Slack channel.

Sends a chat message to the specified channel in the given workspace.
Supports threaded replies via thread_ts.

Args:
    request: Chat request with channel, text, and credentials

Returns:
    ChatResponse with ts, channel, and optional thread_ts

Raises:
    HTTPException:
        - 502 Bad Gateway: If Slack API call fails

get.description:
Find an open merge request by source branch.

Args:
    params: Query parameters with repo_url, source_branch, and token

Returns:
    CreateMergeRequestResponse with the MR URL, or None if not found

parameters.description: Source branch name to search for
parameters.name: source_branch

schema.description: Source branch name to search for
schema.title: Source Branch

post.description:
Create a merge request with file changes in a VCS repository.

Creates a new branch, applies file operations (create/update/delete),
and opens a merge request against the target branch.

Callers should use ``GET /merge-requests`` to check for existing MRs
before calling this endpoint to avoid duplicate creation.

Args:
    request: Merge request details including repo, auth, and file operations

Returns:
    CreateMergeRequestResponse with the URL of the created merge request

get.description:
Get OWNERS file data from a Git repository.

Fetches OWNERS file approvers and reviewers from GitHub or GitLab repositories.
Results are cached for performance (TTL configured in settings).

Args:
    params: VCSQueryParams with repo_url, owners_file, ref, and secret reference

Returns:
    RepoOwnersResponse with provider type, approvers, and reviewers lists

Raises:
    HTTPException:
        - 500 Internal Server Error: If VCS API call fails or tokens not found

Example:
    GET /api/v1/external/vcs/repos/owners?url=https://github.com/openshift/osdctl&path=/&ref=master
    Response:
    {
        "provider": "github",
        "approvers": ["github_user1", "github_user2"],
        "reviewers": ["github_user3"]
    }

parameters.description: Path to OWNERS file in the repository (e.g., /OWNERS or /path/to/OWNERS)
parameters.name: owners_file

schema.description: Path to OWNERS file in the repository (e.g., /OWNERS or /path/to/OWNERS)
schema.title: Owners File

post.description:
Queue creation of a single AWS account.

Queues a background task and returns immediately with a task_id.
The task retries itself until the full workflow completes.
Use GET /create-account/{task_id} to retrieve the result.

Returns 409 with the original task response if a creation workflow
for the same request is already in progress.

parameters.description: Optional: Block up to N seconds for completion.
parameters.name: timeout

schema.description: Optional: Block up to N seconds for completion.
schema.title: Timeout

post.description:
Queue creation of an IAM user in an AWS account.

Assumes into the account via the payer's manager role and creates
an IAM user with the specified policy. Credentials are saved to Vault.
Use GET /create-iam-user/{task_id} to retrieve the result.
post.summary: Aws Account Manager Create Iam User

get.description: Retrieve IAM user creation task result.

parameters.description: Optional: Block up to N seconds for completion.
parameters.name: timeout

schema.description: Optional: Block up to N seconds for completion.
schema.title: Timeout

get.summary: Aws Account Manager Create Iam User Status

post.description:
Queue Glitchtip project alerts reconciliation task.

This endpoint always queues a background task and returns immediately
with a task_id. Use GET /reconcile/{task_id} to retrieve the result.

Args:
    reconcile_request: Reconciliation request with desired state
    current_user: Authenticated user (from JWT token)
    request: FastAPI Request object (used to generate status_url)

Returns:
    GlitchtipProjectAlertsTaskResponse with task_id and status_url

get.description:
Retrieve reconciliation result (blocking or non-blocking).

**Non-blocking mode (default):** Returns immediate status (pending/success/failed)
**Blocking mode (with timeout):** Waits up to timeout seconds, returns 408 if still pending

Args:
    task_id: Task ID from POST /reconcile response
    current_user: Authenticated user (from JWT token)
    timeout: Maximum seconds to wait (default: None = non-blocking)

Returns:
    GlitchtipProjectAlertsTaskResult with status, actions, applied_count, and errors

Raises:
    HTTPException:
        - 404 Not Found: Task ID not found
        - 408 Request Timeout: Task still pending after timeout (blocking mode only)

parameters.description: Optional: Block up to N seconds for completion. Omit for immediate status check.
parameters.name: timeout

schema.description: Optional: Block up to N seconds for completion. Omit for immediate status check.
schema.title: Timeout

post.description:

This endpoint always queues a background task and returns immediately
with a task_id. Use GET /reconcile/{task_id} to retrieve the result.

    current_user: Authenticated user (from JWT token)
    request: FastAPI Request object (used to generate status_url)

Returns:

get.description:
Retrieve reconciliation result (blocking or non-blocking).

**Non-blocking mode (default):** Returns immediate status (pending/success/failed)
**Blocking mode (with timeout):** Waits up to timeout seconds, returns 408 if still pending

Args:

Returns:

        - 408 Request Timeout: Task still pending after timeout (blocking mode only)
